deal.acquirer_legal_counsel: Stikeman Elliott LLP; Willkie Farr & Gallagher LLP

deal_flags.reason: Go-shop contacted 58 potentially interested parties; of the 6 that expressed interest, only Company I executed a CA; the other 5 no-NDA, no-price expressions and the 52 non-responses are §M1 skips.

Saks' simultaneous pursuit of an acquisition of Company B (April–July 2013) is Saks-as-acquirer activity, not a Saks-auction bid. Sponsors C and D (equity financing for Saks' acquisition of Company B) and Company B itself (Saks' acquisition target) are out of Saks-auction scope.

events.comments: Goldman Sachs was Saks' longstanding financial advisor; the earliest narrated advisory engagement in the Background is the December 2012 strategic review.
events.source_quote: For the past several years, Goldman Sachs, one of Saks’ longstanding financial advisors, has participated in these reviews. One such review took place in December 2012.

flags.reason: Filing does not narrate a specific retention date; inferred from first-narrated advisory action (December 2012 strategic review); per §B1 'December 2012' maps to 2012-12-15.
phrase: 'December 2012'

events.comments: Sponsor A's unsolicited phone call expressing interest in a potential acquisition; no price or specific terms.
events.source_quote: In February 2013, Stephen I. Sadove, Saks’ Chairman and Chief Executive Officer, received an unsolicited phone call from a representative of a private equity firm, which we refer to as Sponsor A, expressing interest in a potential acquisition of Saks.

flags.reason: phrase: 'February 2013'

events.comments: Hudson's Bay CEO Richard Baker met with Saks' CEO to discuss a potential acquisition; no price or specific terms.
events.source_quote: On April 1, 2013, Mr. Sadove met with Richard Baker, the Director, Governor, and Chief Executive Officer of Hudson’s Bay, at the request of Mr. Baker and discussed a potential acquisition of Saks by Hudson’s Bay. No specific proposals were made by Hudson’s Bay, and no specific transaction terms were discussed.

flags.reason: §P-R5/§E4: resolved_name="Hudson's Bay Company" for 'bidder_03' not in aliases_observed=["Hudson's Bay"]

events.comments: Board meeting reviewing the Sponsor A and Hudson's Bay expressions of interest and directing continued exploration of potential transactions. This is the board-level resolution that formally opens the sale-exploration process.
events.source_quote: On April 4, 2013, the board held a regularly scheduled telephonic meeting. At this meeting, Mr. Sadove reviewed with the board the expressions of interest in a potential acquisition of Saks from Hudson’s Bay and Sponsor A. The board discussed these expressions of interest, as well as a potential strategic acquisition of Company B. The board directed Mr. Sadove and Saks management to continue exploring these potential transactions.

events.comments: Sponsor E first appears as a potential joint-acquisition partner with Sponsor A; no independent price indication for Sponsor E on this date (the $15 indication is attributed to Hudson's Bay and Sponsor A).
events.source_quote: A private equity firm that we refer to as Sponsor E and that Sponsor A had stated was in discussions with Sponsor A regarding participation in a potential joint acquisition of Saks also participated in the meeting with Sponsor A.

flags.reason: phrase: 'During the week of April 15, 2013'; midpoint: 2013-04-17

events.comments: Pre-NDA concrete price indication (§C4). Sponsor A's NDA is executed on 4/26/2013 (later in same phase), so §P-D6 is satisfied naturally by the later NDA row; no exemption needed.
events.source_quote: During the week of April 15, 2013, representatives of Goldman Sachs met separately with representatives of each of Hudson’s Bay and Sponsor A in order to discuss their respective consideration of a potential acquisition of Saks. Each of Hudson’s Bay and Sponsor A indicated that they were considering making an offer to acquire Saks for at least $15 per share, in cash.

events.comments: Indicative price range communicated through Goldman Sachs.
events.source_quote: During the week of June 3, 2013, representatives of Goldman Sachs spoke with representatives of each of Hudson’s Bay, Sponsor A and Sponsor E and were informed that Hudson’s Bay, on the one hand, and Sponsor A and Sponsor E, on the other hand, were each preliminarily prepared to proceed with the submission of a proposal to acquire Saks at indicative price ranges of $15 to $15.25 per share of common stock (in the case of Hudson’s Bay) and $15 to $16 per share of common stock (in the case of Sponsor A and Sponsor E).

flags.reason: True range $15.00-$15.25/share; informal per §G1 structural signal.

events.comments: Process letter and draft merger agreement distributed to the three contacted counterparties with a July 11 submission deadline — treated as final-round announcement per §K2.
events.source_quote: On July 2, 2013, at the direction of Saks, Goldman Sachs distributed a draft merger agreement and process details to each of Hudson’s Bay, Sponsor A and Sponsor E, with a request for submission of offers for an all-cash acquisition of Saks, along with comments on the draft merger agreement, no later than July 11, 2013.

flags.reason: No explicit 'final round' phrase; inferred per §K2 from process-letter language and explicit July 11 deadline.

events.comments: Sponsor A voluntarily stepped down from primary participation in favor of Sponsor E's new partnership with Sponsor G. Sponsor A later rejoins Sponsor E around July 11 (§I2 re-engagement).
events.source_quote: In early July 2013, Sponsor E informed Saks that Sponsor A was no longer intending to be a primary participant in a potential transaction and that Sponsor E had entered into discussions with Sponsor G, a private equity firm, regarding a potential joint acquisition of Saks.

flags.reason: phrase: 'In early July 2013'

events.comments: Sponsor G CA executed after Sponsor A stepped down and Sponsor E partnered with Sponsor G.
events.source_quote: On July 8, 2013, Saks entered into a confidentiality agreement with Sponsor G.

events.process_conditions_note: Several more weeks of due diligence required; no financing documentation; no revised merger agreement.
events.comments: Joint Sponsor E + Sponsor G final-round bid: $14.50-$15.50 range (informal per §G1 structural range signal), no merger-agreement markup, no financing support — per-constituent row per §E2.
events.source_quote: The joint proposal from Sponsor E and Sponsor G included an indicative price range of $14.50–$15.50 per share of common stock, but noted that the parties would require several weeks to continue their due diligence evaluation in order to submit a more definitive proposal and did not include a revised draft merger agreement or any documents supporting availability of financing for the proposal.

flags.reason: True range $14.50-$15.50/share; informal per §G1 structural signal.

events.process_conditions_note: Several more weeks of due diligence required; no financing documentation; no revised merger agreement.
events.comments: Joint Sponsor E + Sponsor G final-round bid; per-constituent row per §E2.
events.source_quote: The joint proposal from Sponsor E and Sponsor G included an indicative price range of $14.50–$15.50 per share of common stock, but noted that the parties would require several weeks to continue their due diligence evaluation in order to submit a more definitive proposal and did not include a revised draft merger agreement or any documents supporting availability of financing for the proposal.

flags.reason: True range $14.50-$15.50/share; informal per §G1 structural signal.

events.bid_type_inference_note: Formal per §G1 triggers: (a) 'committed debt and equity financing' (financing commitments); (b) 'revised draft merger agreement' (markup of the merger agreement); and (c) §G1 process-position — bid submitted in direct response to the July 2 process letter / final-round invitation.
events.comments: Hudson's Bay final-round bid: $15.25/share with committed debt and equity financing and revised draft merger agreement — classified formal per §G1 triggers (committed financing + markup of merger agreement) and process-position (submitted in response to the July 2 process letter).
events.source_quote: On July 11, 2013, each of Hudson’s Bay, on the one hand, and Sponsor E, together with Sponsor G, on the other hand, submitted proposals expressing their continued interest in an acquisition of Saks. Hudson’s Bay’s proposal included a price of $15.25 per share of common stock, a revised draft merger agreement and information and documentation relating to Hudson’s Bay’s committed debt and equity financing for the potential transaction.

flags.reason: §P-R5/§E4: resolved_name="Hudson's Bay Company" for 'bidder_03' not in aliases_observed=["Hudson's Bay"]

events.comments: Sponsor G voluntarily withdrew shortly after the July 11 joint bid; Sponsor A rejoined Sponsor E as primary participant. The filing narrates this 'subsequently' after the July 11 submissions and before the next dated event (week of July 15); attributing to July 11 as the narrated day of the withdrawal.
events.source_quote: Saks was subsequently informed that Sponsor G was no longer participating in the process, and that Sponsor E would again be joined in its proposal by Sponsor A as a primary participant.

flags.reason: Filing narrates withdrawal 'subsequently' after July 11 submissions and before July 15-week events; anchored to July 11 per §B3 context anchor.

events.bid_type_inference_note: Informal per §G1 process-position fallback: unsolicited first-contact letter, no NDA, no financing documentation, no merger-agreement markup, 'with no details or further information' — not a binding proposal. §D1.a first-contact exempts from §P-D6.
events.additional_note: unsolicited letter; aggregate $2.6B, no per-share, no details
events.comments: Unsolicited first-contact letter from Company H with an aggregate $2.6 billion cash offer; Goldman Sachs attempted contact multiple times without success and no further communication from Company H. Classified informal per §G1 process-position fallback (pre-process-letter for this bidder; no NDA; no financing documentation; not a binding proposal).
events.source_quote: On July 21, 2013, Saks received a letter from Company H, a privately held company based in the U.S. unknown to Saks and its advisors, purporting to propose to acquire Saks for an aggregate price of $2.6 billion in cash, with no details or further information. Goldman Sachs subsequently attempted on more than one occasion to contact the appropriate person at Company H both by telephone and by e-mail to discuss the purported offer further but was unsuccessful in making contact with such person. Neither Saks nor Goldman Sachs received any subsequent communications from Company H.

flags.reason: §D1.a: unsolicited first-contact Bid, no NDA in phase, Goldman attempted contact 'but was unsuccessful' and 'Neither Saks nor Goldman Sachs received any subsequent communications from Company H'.

events.process_conditions_note: Subject to negotiating a definitive, binding agreement; agreeable to Saks' position on substantially all significant merger agreement and financing issues.
events.bid_type_inference_note: Formal per §G1 triggers: 'subject to negotiating a definitive, binding agreement' (binding-offer language); 'committed financing of the merger' (financing commitments); and §G1 process-position — revised bid in the final-round negotiation window immediately before signing.
events.comments: Hudson's Bay revised formal bid at $16/share; §G1 formal triggers (committed debt and equity financing carried over from July 11 proposal; active merger-agreement negotiation).
events.source_quote: On July 24, 2013, representatives of Hudson’s Bay advised Goldman Sachs that, subject to negotiating a definitive, binding agreement, Hudson’s Bay was prepared to offer $16 per share of common stock and was agreeable to Saks’ position on substantially all of the significant issues in the draft merger agreement and the draft agreements relating to Hudson’s Bay’s committed financing of the merger conveyed by Goldman Sachs to Hudson’s Bay.

events.comments: Morgan Stanley described as 'a long-time advisor to Saks' first narrated in the Background on July 26, 2013; no earlier retention date stated.
events.source_quote: Also on July 26, 2013, the board held a special telephonic meeting attended by representatives of Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley & Co. LLC (a long-time advisor to Saks, referred to as “Morgan Stanley”) and Wachtell Lipton.

flags.reason: Filing does not narrate Morgan Stanley's retention date; anchored to earliest Background mention (July 26, 2013).

events.bid_date_rough: implicit drop at process end: 2013-07-28 board approval of Hudson's Bay merger
events.comments: Implicit drop: the Sponsor A/E joint bid capped at $15.50 vs Hudson's Bay's $16; by approving the Hudson's Bay merger on July 28, the board implicitly terminated the Sponsor A/E alternative. Split per §I1 to Sponsor A and Sponsor E individually since each signed its own NDA on 4/26.
events.source_quote: In the evening of July 23, 2013, senior management of Saks met with representatives of Sponsor A and Sponsor E in connection with their evaluation of an acquisition of Saks. There was no indication from Sponsor A or Sponsor E that they were prepared to increase their indicated price beyond their initially indicated range of $14.50 to $15.50 per share of common stock, nor did it appear likely that Sponsor A and Sponsor E would be prepared to conclude their diligence more quickly than they had previously indicated.

flags.reason: Filing does not narrate an explicit drop statement; inferred from board's July 28 approval of Hudson's Bay merger and the July 23 evening meeting at which Sponsor A/E would not match.
Sponsor A/E joint proposal dropped; row split per constituent NDA (Sponsor A and Sponsor E each signed individually on 4/26).

events.bid_date_rough: implicit drop at process end: 2013-07-28 board approval of Hudson's Bay merger
events.comments: Implicit drop of Sponsor A/E joint bid; per-constituent row per §I1 consortium-drop split.
events.source_quote: In the evening of July 23, 2013, senior management of Saks met with representatives of Sponsor A and Sponsor E in connection with their evaluation of an acquisition of Saks. There was no indication from Sponsor A or Sponsor E that they were prepared to increase their indicated price beyond their initially indicated range of $14.50 to $15.50 per share of common stock, nor did it appear likely that Sponsor A and Sponsor E would be prepared to conclude their diligence more quickly than they had previously indicated.

flags.reason: Filing does not narrate an explicit drop statement; inferred from board's July 28 approval of Hudson's Bay merger.

events.process_conditions_note: 40-day go-shop provision; $73.5M target termination fee ($40.1M during go-shop-related terminations); $173.8M reverse termination fee; all-cash $16/share.
events.comments: Merger agreement signed July 28, 2013 at $16/share cash. Public announcement press release issued July 29, 2013 per the §'Sale Press Release' convention folded into the Executed row.
source_quote: Following the board’s approval of the merger and the merger agreement, Saks, Hudson’s Bay and Merger Sub finalized and executed the merger agreement and other transaction documents later on July 28, 2013.
On July 29, 2013, Saks and Hudson’s Bay issued a joint press release announcing entry into the transaction.

events.comments: Joint announcement press release by Saks and Hudson's Bay.
events.source_quote: On July 29, 2013, Saks and Hudson’s Bay issued a joint press release announcing entry into the transaction.

events.bid_date_rough: approximately two weeks after the merger agreement was executed
events.comments: Go-shop NDA. Of 58 parties contacted by Goldman Sachs during the go-shop, 6 expressed interest and only Company I executed a CA. Filing locates the CA 'approximately two weeks after the merger agreement was executed' on 7/28/2013, so inferred to 2013-08-12 per §B1 anchored phrase (anchor 7/28 + ~14 days).
events.source_quote: Company I had not signed a confidentiality agreement until approximately two weeks after the merger agreement was executed, that Company I had been given access to the same information that was provided to Hudson’s Bay and other third parties with whom Saks had held discussions (as well as certain requested information that had not been (but then was) provided to Hudson’s Bay) and that Company I had not requested a meeting with, or access to, any member of management of Saks.

flags.reason: Filing states Company I's CA was executed 'approximately two weeks after the merger agreement was executed' (7/28/2013); inferred to 2013-08-12.

events.comments: Go-shop ended September 6, 2013 with no party designated as an excluded party; Company I did not submit an acquisition proposal before the go-shop deadline and subsequently only corresponded about the go-shop's duration rather than bidding. Row aligns to end-of-go-shop as the Drop date.
events.source_quote: The go shop period ended on September 6, 2013, and no party has been designated by Saks as an excluded party. During the go shop process, Goldman Sachs, on behalf of Saks, contacted 58 potentially interested third parties, including private equity firms, companies involved in the retail industry and other potential acquirors. Of those contacted, only six parties expressed interest, and only one of the six (which we refer to as Company I) executed a confidentiality agreement with, and conducted a due diligence investigation of, Saks. None of the parties contacted as part of the go shop process, including Company I, has submitted an acquisition proposal for Saks.
